prompt:
We bridge a handful of edge services into Kafka through the Confluent REST Proxy because they can't link a native client, and the shell-script producer we use today throws away per-record results - when half a batch fails we either re-send everything (duplicates) or nothing (data loss). I want a real Go package for this.

Non-negotiable first step: before changing code, research the CURRENT official Confluent REST Proxy docs on docs.confluent.io with WebSearch and WebFetch - the API reference and the quickstart. The proxy has BOTH a v2 and a v3 produce surface; work out from the official pages which one this producer needs (batch request, per-record offsets/errors array, embedded-format media types, and the documented rule about excluding schema fields when keys/values are null) and say explicitly in your final answer which version you picked and why. Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json, provenance in docs/official_sources.json - both protected. If the current official docs contradict the pinned fixture, report the conflict instead of editing fixtures or tests. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final response.

Deliverable: package restproduce (module already set up), implementing at least:

- NewClient(baseURL string, hc *http.Client, username, password string) *Client - Basic auth on every request; never follow ANY redirect (a 3xx is an error, credentials must not travel to another host).
- Types: Format (json/avro/binary/jsonschema/protobuf), Record{Key, Value json.RawMessage; Partition *int32} (nil member = omit it from the wire), BatchRequest{Format; KeySchema, ValueSchema string}, BatchResult{KeySchemaID, ValueSchemaID *int32; Results []RecordResult}, RecordResult{Partition *int32; Offset *int64; ErrorCode *int64; Error string} with Succeeded() and Retriable() methods, APIError{HTTPStatus int; ErrorCode int64; Message string}.
- Produce(ctx, topic, req, records) (*BatchResult, error): correct endpoint + Content-Type for the format, Accept header per the docs, schema fields only when the format takes them AND the docs' null-key/null-value exclusion rule says they may be sent, decode per-record outcomes positionally, HTTP 200 with failed records is NOT a Go error, request-level envelopes ({error_code, message}) become *APIError. Reject an empty batch locally.
- ProduceWithRetry(ctx, topic, req, records, maxAttempts int, backoff func(attempt int)) (*BatchResult, error): re-send ONLY records the proxy marked retriable, merge outcomes back into their original positions, never re-send successes or non-retriable failures, call the injected backoff between attempts (no time.Sleep anywhere), and honor ctx cancellation between attempts while preserving the partial result.

Acceptance: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... (loopback fake proxy, dummy creds). produce_test.go and docs/ are protected - don't touch them. Structure the package however you like beyond that; I'd rather have clean request-building/decoding/retry layers than one giant function.

research.interface: Confluent REST Proxy API v2 produce endpoint (Confluent Platform, current docs; v3 evaluated and documented as the alternative)

official_sources.title: API Reference for Confluent REST Proxy
official_sources.used_for: v2 produce contract: POST /topics/{topic_name}; per-format Content-Type media types (application/vnd.kafka.json.v2+json, .avro.v2+json, .binary.v2+json, .jsonschema.v2+json, .protobuf.v2+json); request fields key_schema/key_schema_id/value_schema/value_schema_id/records with the rule that null keys/values must exclude the corresponding schema fields; response offsets array with partition/offset/error_code/error; error_code 1 = non-retriable vs 2 = retriable; HTTP 200 with per-record failures; 404/40401 and 422/42202 envelopes; v2 vs v3 versioning and the v3 application/json media type

official_sources.title: Quick Start for Confluent REST Proxy
official_sources.used_for: exact v2 produce request/response examples: json produce with Content-Type application/vnd.kafka.json.v2+json and response {"offsets":[{"partition":0,"offset":0,"error_code":null,"error":null}],"key_schema_id":null,"value_schema_id":null}; avro produce sending value_schema and receiving value_schema_id 21; keyed avro produce sending both key_schema and value_schema; Accept: application/vnd.kafka.v2+json

research.language_rationale: Confluent ships no official Go SDK for the REST Proxy; the documented integration surface is versioned HTTP+JSON media types, and Go's net/http + encoding/json is the idiomatic zero-dependency stack for a sidecar producer that bridges services without native Kafka clients.
